laptop.name: Laptop Computer
laptop.fact: The first laptop weighed 10.7kg and cost $1,795. Today there are over 2 billion laptops in use worldwide.

sunglasses.name: Sunglasses
sunglasses.nutrition: Blocks 99–100% of UV-A and UV-B radiation
sunglasses.fact: The Inuit invented the first sunglasses from walrus ivory with narrow slits to reduce glare from snow — long before plastic existed.

printer.name: Printer
printer.fact: The first inkjet printer was invented by Hewlett-Packard in 1976. A single ink cartridge costs more per milliliter than vintage champagne.

photocopier.name: Photocopier
photocopier.nutrition: Uses xerography — static electricity and heat
photocopier.fact: The first photocopier took 3 minutes to make a single copy. Chester Carlson invented xerography in 1938 but was rejected by IBM and General Electric before Xerox took it on.

cellphone.name: Mobile Phone
cellphone.fact: The first mobile phone call was made by Martin Cooper of Motorola in 1973. The phone weighed 1.1kg and took 10 hours to charge for 30 minutes of talk time.

remote.name: Remote Control
remote.nutrition: Powered by AA or AAA batteries
remote.fact: The first TV remote was called the Lazy Bones and was connected to the TV by a wire. The first wireless remote used flashlight beams — it accidentally changed channels when sunlight hit the TV.

iPod.name: iPod
iPod.fact: Steve Jobs described the original iPod as 1000 songs in your pocket. Apple sold over 450 million iPods before discontinuing it in 2022.

joystick.name: Joystick / Game Controller
joystick.nutrition: Powered by USB or wireless receiver
joystick.fact: The word joystick originally referred to aircraft control sticks in WWI. Atari popularized it for gaming in 1977 with the iconic CX40 joystick.

flower.name: Yellow Flower
flower.nutrition: Supports pollinators like bees and butterflies
flower.fact: Yellow flowers get their color from pigments called carotenoids — the same pigments that make carrots orange. Bees are especially attracted to yellow flowers because they can see ultraviolet light.
